Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First understand the DECLARE model It is a process model using DECLARE constraints The model includes init R existence C responded existence between E and A precedence C requires R before and noncoexistence between E and C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The last constraint is a problem because the intended process includes both Evaluate and Close Indeed the intended flow has both E and C So noncoexistence E and C directly contradicts the existence of both E and C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also have existence C So if a trace has C must have C then noncoexistence says E cannot exist so E must not be present That means the process cannot have both Evaluate and Close but the intended process requires both

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The responded existence if E occurs then A occurs is fine because in intended flow A occurs before E

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model allows skipping of critical steps Evaluate Approve Notify and even Assign in some cases since no existence requirement for A only if E appears So a trace could be R C or R A C and still satisfy the constraints

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Trace R C would satisfy init R existence C precedence C responded existence no E so vacuously true and noncoexistence no E so true

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus anomalies Direct contradiction existence of C and noncoexistence between E and C forces E to never occur which contradicts the intended process that includes Evaluate

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Misinterpretation of business requirements maybe they thought that evaluation and closure are mutually exclusive Or maybe they intended noncoexistence between some other activities but mis specified

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Incremental changes maybe initially evaluation was not required before closure but later they added evaluation but forgot to remove noncoexistence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Technical issues maybe the DECLARE model was automatically mined from event logs and due to incomplete or noisy data it produced contradictory constraints

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Pressure to handle claims quickly perhaps they wanted to allow skipping evaluation to close claims quickly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Check for claims that have a Close event but no Evaluate event

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Check for claims that have both Evaluate and Close events violating noncoexistence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Check for claims that have an Evaluate event but no Assign event violating responded existence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Check for claims that have a Close event but no preceding Receive event violating precedence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Check for claims that have no Close event violating existence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Additional check ordering between Assign and Evaluate to see if there are Evaluate events without prior Assign even though responded existence does not require ordering but the intended flow expects A before E
